metadata.districtModel: five sections; five district slots per section; D13 is the seat of power

cityDNA.coreFantasy: A amphibian frog/tadpole folk city where Polliweir Frog-Dam Choir of the Living Marsh, Lock House Beacon, Reed Bundle Patterns Sovereign Registry Court, and the five-section district spine turn local identity into functional civic space.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Pollyr Fen should feel immediately identifiable through exact landmarks and materials, not through generic fantasy-city shorthand.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Pollyr Fen organizes power through Reed Bundle Patterns Sovereign Registry Court, public response through Lock House Beacon, civic memory through Polliweir Frog-Dam Choir of the Living Marsh, and daily life through S1 Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, S2 Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, S3 Civic Services Hall, S4 Medical / Healing Civic Hall, S5 Granary / Food Market Civic Hall.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Cedar, Thatch, Malachite, Resin, Painted Plaster, Riverside, Floodplain, Canal Basin, Beast Bonding, Ancestor Guidance, River Blessing, and exact anchors including Reed Bundle Patterns Sovereign Registry Court, Lock House Beacon, Polliweir Frog-Dam Choir of the Living Marsh, Grain Hall, Nursery Basin, Healing Hall, Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, Canal Reflections Monument, Waterwheel Court Nook, Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A amphibian frog/tadpole folk civic culture where architecture, public obligations, roads, landmark approaches, and everyday work must all read as part of the same civilization.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city symbol system is built from exact names and visible functions: Polliweir Frog-Dam Choir of the Living Marsh, Lock House Beacon, Reed Bundle Patterns Sovereign Registry Court, and section anchors such as Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, Civic Services Hall, Medical / Healing Civic Hall, Granary / Food Market Civic Hall.

canonLocks: Pollyr Fen uses city code PFN.
The Wonder is Polliweir Frog-Dam Choir of the Living Marsh.
The Beacon is Lock House Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Reed Bundle Patterns Sovereign Registry Court and occupies District 13.
The city uses five sections with five tactical district slots per section.

The source-backed section spine is: S1 Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, S2 Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, S3 Civic Services Hall, S4 Medical / Healing Civic Hall, S5 Granary / Food Market Civic Hall.

rejectedReads: Do not treat Pollyr Fen as a generic fantasy settlement with renamed landmarks.
Do not collapse the five-section board into a single mood or single district.
Do not replace exact anchor names with generic summaries.
Do not invent missing street-section landmarks when a street source is absent.
cautionFlags: Keep every named Wonder, Beacon, Seat, section civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook exact when present in sidecars.
If an image appears to deviate from a sidecar, preserve the exact name but avoid overclaiming visual details not supported by the source package.
Every district asset should remain usable for narrative scenes, tactical board dioramas, and city map backgrounds.

environment.environmentalLogic: The environment should make Pollyr Fen's exact civic anchors plausible at street scale: terrain routes people toward section landmarks, lighting clarifies public authority, and water or landscape features remain source-backed rather than decorative filler.

civilization.governance: Government is centered on Reed Bundle Patterns Sovereign Registry Court. Its authority should be visible through courts, records, public access routes, civic lamps, guards or attendants, and the way citizens respond to D13.

streets.appearance: Streets should show Pollyr Fen's exact anchor system at pedestrian scale: S1 Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, S2 Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, S3 Civic Services Hall, S4 Medical / Healing Civic Hall, S5 Granary / Food Market Civic Hall. Keep roads, plazas, stairs, thresholds, lamps, signs-without-readable-text, props, and civilians aligned to the source-backed materials and landmarks.
streets.movement: Movement should be readable for tactical-board assets: citizens pass between 25 district slots, section anchors, D13, service streets, and landmark approaches without visual clutter hiding the civic purpose.
sounds: landmark-specific sounds from water, bells, animals, machines, or crowds when source-backed
smells: worn stone, timber, metal, plaster, or fabric
streets.lighting: Use the package's source-backed civic lighting, daylight/golden-hour reads, lanterns, and beacon glow. Lighting must help identify authority, safety, and landmark function.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should reinforce the local terrain and materials without overwhelming the city identity or inventing unsupported climate extremes.
streets.dayCycle: Day scenes emphasize errands, trade, records, repairs, rituals, food, and section-level movement.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes emphasize lantern routes, closed thresholds, guarded landmarks, quiet work, and beacon visibility.

common: street services tied to section anchors
exports: specialized tools or ritual goods as supported by anchors
imports: dyes, oils, spices, timber, stone, metal, glass, or cloth as needed by the architecture

housing.wealthy: Prestige housing clusters near Reed Bundle Patterns Sovereign Registry Court, high terraces, or landmark-facing routes, using the best source-backed materials and controlled views of civic space.
housing.middle: Shop-over-house units, courtyard homes, guild-adjacent apartments, and family rooms near section anchors.
housing.poor: Smaller back-street rooms, work-adjacent housing, shared courtyards, service alleys, patched materials, and dense but still city-specific lived-in detail.
